  "findings": [
    "Insufficient anchor bolt projection above the nut",
    "Non-compliance with API RP 686 installation standards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current anchor bolt installation",
    "Perform engineering evaluation to determine if thread engagement is sufficient or if coupling/extension is required",
    "Re-install or extend anchor bolts to achieve the minimum required projection (typically minimum 1.5 threads or 1/4 inch past the nut)"
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 686 requires proper anchor bolt projection (typically a minimum of 1.5 threads or 1/4 inch beyond the top of the nut) to ensure full thread engagement and structural integrity of the machinery mounting. Insufficient projection is a non-compliant condition that can lead to loose anchor bolts, excessive vibration, and equipment damage during operation.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 4"
  ]
}
